subject: History
overall_learning_objectives: Understand how new dynasties emerged through Samantas and Chieftains
Learn about the Tripartite Struggle for control over Kanauj
Identify the importance of Prashastis and Land Grants
Understand the nature of warfare, including the raids of Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni
Learn about the administration and temple-building of the Chola Empire
Recognize the unique irrigation and agricultural methods of the Cholas
Identify the function of the Sabha in Chola village administration

lesson_plan.title: The Rise of New Dynasties
topics_covered: Who were Samantas? (Big landlords/warriors)
How Samantas became powerful (Maha-samanta)
The story of Dantidurga and the Hiranya-garbha ritual
teaching_strategy: Analogy: A school principal and their coordinators (Samantas)
Storytelling: How the Rashtrakutas overthrew the Chalukyas
Explanation of 'Hiranya-garbha' (The Golden Womb) in simple terms
student_activities: Draw a crown and write the names of three new dynasties (Rashtrakutas, Gurjara-Pratiharas, Palas)
Discuss: 'Can a non-Kshatriya become a king?'
assessment: Which dynasty did Dantidurga belong to?

lesson_plan.title: Kings and Their Administration
topics_covered: High-sounding titles (Maharaja-adhiraja)
How kings shared power with peasants and traders
Types of taxes (Vetti and Kadamai)
teaching_strategy: Discussion: 'If you were a king, what long name would you give yourself?'
Explaining taxes: Vetti (forced labor) vs. Kadamai (land revenue)
Flowchart: Money from taxes $ightarrow$ Building temples and forts
student_activities: List two things kings did with the tax money they collected
Role-play: A tax collector talking to a farmer
assessment: Who were the resources obtained from in the villages?

lesson_plan.title: Prashastis and Land Grants
topics_covered: What are Prashastis? (In praise of kings)
Copper plates as records of land grants
The unusual poem by Kalhana about Kashmir
teaching_strategy: Showing pictures of copper plates from the textbook
Explaining that Prashastis were not always 100% true
Comparison: Writing a 'good report card' for a king
student_activities: Imagine you are a poet; write two lines praising your favorite leader
Find the word 'Brahmanas' and explain their role in writing Prashastis
assessment: Oral quiz: What language were most Prashastis written in?
Why were land grants recorded on copper plates?

lesson_plan.title: Warfare for Wealth
topics_covered: The Tripartite Struggle for Kanauj
Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni and his raids on Somnath
Prithviraj Chauhan and Muhammad Ghori (Battles of Tarain)
teaching_strategy: Map work: Locating Kanauj and the three fighting dynasties
Storytelling: Why Mahmud of Ghazni wanted the wealth of Indian temples
Simple timeline of the First and Second Battles of Tarain
student_activities: Mark the route of Mahmud of Ghazni on a map of India
Identify the year of the First Battle of Tarain
assessment: Which three dynasties fought the 'Tripartite Struggle'?

lesson_plan.title: The Chola Empire: Rise to Power
topics_covered: How the Cholas rose (Muttarayar to Vijayalaya)
The great rulers: Rajaraja I and Rajendra I
The expansion of the Chola kingdom
teaching_strategy: Storytelling: Vijayalaya capturing Thanjavur
Highlighting the Chola Navy and overseas conquests
Visuals of the Kaveri delta
student_activities: Write two sentences about Rajaraja I
assessment: Who was the founder of the Chola dynasty?
Who is considered the most powerful Chola ruler?

lesson_plan.title: Chola Temples and Agriculture
topics_covered: Temples of Thanjavur and Gangaikonda-cholapuram
Bronze images (The famous Chola art)
Making the Kaveri delta fertile (Embankments and Canals)
teaching_strategy: Showing pictures of the Big Temple and Bronze Nataraja
Explaining how temples were centers of social life, not just worship
Diagram: How canals bring water to the fields
student_activities: Draw a simple canal system for a field
Discuss: 'Why did kings build such big temples?'
assessment: What was the Chola bronze work famous for?
Name one way the Cholas improved irrigation

lesson_plan.title: Chola Administration and Review
topics_covered: Ur (Village) and Nadu (Groups of villages)
The Sabha (Committee for Brahmanas)
The Uttaramerur inscription
teaching_strategy: Explaining the 'Lottery System' used to pick committee members
Reviewing key terms from the whole chapter
NCERT back-exercise discussion
student_activities: Role-play: Picking a name from a pot (the Sabha lottery)
Complete the chapter keyword matching chart

teaching_aids: NCERT History Textbook: Our Pasts - II
Pictures of Chola Bronze statues and temples
Photos of Copper Plate land grants
A small pot and slips of paper for the 'Sabha' role-play
evaluation_methods: Map marking of Kanauj and Thanjavur
teacher_tip: Focus on the 'Sabha' lottery system in the last period. Kids love the idea that 1000 years ago, people were chosen for committees by picking names out of a pot, just like they do today for games!
